## prompt-6-full-bill

*Generated by Process A from `auto-bill-01/sub-prompts/prompt-6-full-bill.md`
and `cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/full-bill/prompt-full-bill.md`, adapted for
the single-prompt v5.0 financial-data build.*

Use `auto-bill-02/draft-bill` and its bracketed [DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS] to
generate the full H. R. 9510 Bill v5.0 LaTeX and the corresponding zip into
`auto-bill-02/full-bill`. Execute every bracketed instruction: render each
gray-scale Mermaid figure as a gray-scale TikZ figure, set each ASCII figure in
its centered white-background monospace frame, fill in each full-width table
with its financial data (fiscal-year series right-aligned in `R` columns,
dollar figures marked illustrative unless tied to a cited statute or notice),
carry the operative text so the bill stands on its own, and write the five
financial appendices in full LaTeX, each readable to legislators and each
represented in the SECTION 1(b) table of contents. The "(b) Table of Contents"
must fill the remainder of its page with clickable entries, each linking to its
part. Utilize the DOI `10.5281/zenodo.xxxxxxxx`
([https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.xxxxxxxx](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.xxxxxxxx))
for this v5.0 bill (Rule 15).

Each figure must be centered and weighted correctly, just as it would appear in
a GitHub Markdown block. No PNG or JPG images; tables, ASCII, and gray-scale
Mermaid (as TikZ) only; white background throughout. Comprehensively populate,
refine, and polish the `.tex` files, `.bib`, `.sty`, and README, all compiling
in Overleaf. The bill keeps its 2026-specific name, reads as if teams of top
human authors wrote it, and does not lose specifics from the draft while
remaining readable. Double-check and correct bill, law, and budget-term names
(budget authority versus outlays; direct versus discretionary spending; the
PAYGO scorecard; the MDUFA fee classes). Keep the bill (a) current with
medical-AI and budget law, (b) grounded in mass-adopted references, (c)
relevant to emerging bills only as research influences in Appendix D, and (d)
streamlined.

One commit each for `main.tex`, `usctitle.sty`, `references.bib`, and
`README.md`; one commit per `sections/*.tex` - the five financial appendices
are processed as separate tasks apart from the bill body and submitted as
individual commits in real time. For the second-to-last commit, fix all errors
across all files. For the last commit, write the directory README (DOI badges,
repository structure, gray-scale Mermaid diagram, figure and table inventory
split by medium) and the `full-bill-LaTeX.zip`. All commits belong to stage
milestone M7 of the single pull request.

Formatting: avoid large empty white spaces; even interword spacing; no text
running off the right margin; avoid lines with a single or two words; every
table matches the body width using `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}`
or `R{<width>}` columns; avoid a single line stranded on the next page; correct
"SS" into "§"; single hyphens only; senior-author `\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and
`\hspace` page balancing. Do not overwrite or change anything in
`auto-bill-02/draft-bill`.

Create `auto-bill-02/full-bill/prompt-full-bill.md` (this prompt verbatim under
a `## prompt-full-bill` heading) and `output-full-bill.md` (the narrative
output). Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode. The LaTeX must
compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX.
